articles.headline: Portland visits Washington after Iriafen's 25-point outing

articles.headline: Mystics power past short-handed Tempo 79-62 behind double-doubles by Iriafen and Austin
articles.description: — Kiki Iriafen had 25 points and 14 rebounds, Shakira Austin added 17 points and 10 rebounds, and the Washington Mystics beat the Toronto Tempo 79-62 on Tuesday night.

articles.headline: Man fired after alleged racist message to Aces' Chelsea Gray
articles.description: Hilton Grand Vacations said Tuesday that it has fired a man who allegedly sent a racist message to Aces guard Chelsea Gray on social media.

articles.headline: Aaliyah Edwards and Brittney Griner each score 20-plus to lead the Sun past the Fire 90-87
articles.description: — Aaliyah Edwards scored 21 points off the bench, Brittney Griner added 20, and the Connecticut Sun beat the Portland Fire 90-87 on Tuesday.

articles.headline: Paige Bueckers bemoans WNBA's lack of Black women head coaches
articles.description: Wings star Paige Bueckers called out the lack of Black women head coaches in the WNBA, saying Black women deserve "the same equal opportunity" to coach in the league.

images.name: Paige Bueckers [1296x729]
images.caption: Paige Bueckers is averaging 19.8 points and 5.8 assists but is day-to-day because of a right ankle injury and missed Saturday's loss at Portland.

articles.headline: Los Angeles visits Minnesota following McBride's 37-point game
articles.description: Los Angeles Sparks (10-12, 5-6 Western Conference) at Minnesota Lynx (18-6, 11-1 Western Conference)

articles.headline: Lynx rookie Miles reaches 400 points, 100 rebounds, 100 assists
articles.description: Minnesota rookie Olivia Miles scored a career-high 33, becoming the first WNBA player to reach 400 points, 100 rebounds and 100 assists in only 22 career games, and the Lynx defeated the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 Monday night for a third straight victory.

images.name: Olivia Miles scores career-high 33 points in Lynx's win over the Mercury
images.caption: Olivia Miles scores career-high 33 points in Lynx's win over the Mercury

articles.headline: Kayla McBride, Olivia Miles star in the Lynx's 104-100 win over the Mercury
articles.description: — Kayla McBride scored a season-high 37 points, rookie Olivia Miles had a career-high 33 and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 on Monday night for their third straight victory.

article.headline: Jessica Shepard secures 3rd triple-double of the season and the Wings beat the Liberty 88-77
article.description: — Jessica Shepard had 22 points, 12 rebounds and 11 assists for her third triple-double of the season, and the Dallas Wings beat the New York Liberty 88-77 on Tuesday night.
article.linkText: Jessica Shepard secures 3rd triple-double of the season and the Wings beat the Liberty 88-77

images.name: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season
images.caption: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season

video.headline: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season
video.shortHeadline: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season
video.caption: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season
video.title: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season
video.description: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season
video.shortDescription: Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Jessica Shepard gets her 3rd triple-double of the season) 2026/07/08 ESHEET

article.story: NEW YORK -- — Jessica Shepard had 22 points, 12 rebounds and 11 assists for her third triple-double of the season, and the Dallas Wings beat the New York Liberty 88-77 on Tuesday night. Dallas (14-8) earned its eighth road victory of the season — after totaling just six over the past two seasons. Paige Bueckers made her only 3-pointer of the game with 3:12 left in the fourth quarter to give Dallas an 82-70 lead. Shepard was credited with an assist on the play to become the second player in WNBA history with three triple-doubles in a season, joining Alyssa Thomas. Shepard's fourth career triple-double moved her into a tie with Sabrina Ionescu for second-most behind Thomas. Bueckers finished with 15 points, seven rebounds and six assists for Dallas, which has won five of its last seven games. Azzi Fudd scored 12 points and Awak Kuier had 10 points, 12 rebounds and seven assists. Breanna Stewart had 29 points and nine rebounds for New York (13-9), which has lost two home games to Dallas this season. Jonquel Jones added 17 points and Ionescu scored 14. Stewart made four of New York’s 10 field goals in the first half, while the rest of her teammates combined to go 6 of 27 from the field to trail Dallas 47-31. Up next Dallas: At Toronto on Friday. New York: At Minnesota on Saturday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
